methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $250M last post money.

data.narrative:
Vulcan Elements is a Series A materials company founded by John Maslin and Piotr Kulik. The company has raised $65M to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $250M. No public or private comparable companies were available for direct benchmarking within the materials sector.

Given the limited comp universe, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. This approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $250M Series A post-money valuation, reflecting typical progression economics from Series A to subsequent funding rounds. This method yields an implied valuation of $500M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $425M to $575M, with a median of $500M. The range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression result but remains anchored to a single applicable method. The primary uncertainty is the absence of sector peers—either public or private—that would permit revenue multiple, margin comparison, or growth-rate triangulation. Any refinement of this range would require either identification of comparable materials-stage companies or access to company-specific metrics (revenue, growth rate, unit economics) that were not provided in the available data.
